verdict_rationale: No blockers found. 16 is a sound index: mergeability ordering, trust-tier semantics, and scope boundaries are all clear. 16a has well-structured TDD discipline and correctly absorbed its rev-2 blocker (quarantine inference). Four changes requested before merge: (1) fix a factual error in the Task 5 commit message that contradicts the plan's own rev-2 history; (2) add test coverage for the two untested Determinism derivation cases; (3) add boundary-value tests for data_trust_tier; (4) add the plugin protocols to the mypy sweep so type coverage matches the implementation discipline. All are MINOR or NIT. No security issues, no defensive programming violations, no tier-model violations.

warnings.title: SEEDED and NON_DETERMINISTIC derivation cases not tested
warnings.detail: The _DETERMINISM_TO_AUDIT_FLAG table at line 780 of the plan maps all six Determinism enum values (IO_READ, IO_WRITE, EXTERNAL_CALL, DETERMINISTIC, SEEDED, NON_DETERMINISTIC) to audit-characteristic strings. The test suite (lines 664-751) covers IO_READ (_FakeSource), EXTERNAL_CALL (_FakeTransformWithNetwork), DETERMINISTIC (_FakeTransformDeterministic), IO_WRITE (_FakeSink) — four of six. SEEDED and NON_DETERMINISTIC have no test case. The derivation is a simple dict lookup so the risk of a regression is low, but the pattern in this plan is thorough per-enum coverage, and both values exist in the codebase (e.g., LLM transforms use EXTERNAL_CALL; a hypothetical seeded source would use SEEDED). Add two fixture classes and two test functions asserting 'seeded' and 'non_deterministic' appear in the derived tuple respectively.
evidence: 16a-phase-7a-backend.md lines 632-655: five fixture classes, none using Determinism.SEEDED or Determinism.NON_DETERMINISTIC
src/elspeth/contracts/enums.py lines 122-127: all six Determinism values

warnings.title: data_trust_tier validation tests missing boundary values and negative cases
warnings.detail: test_plugin_summary_rejects_invalid_trust_tier at line 459 tests data_trust_tier=7 (above upper bound). The plan's schema uses Field(ge=1, le=3). Missing tests: (a) data_trust_tier=0 — at the ge=1 boundary, should reject; (b) data_trust_tier=-1 — negative, should reject; (c) data_trust_tier=1 — minimum valid, should accept; (d) data_trust_tier=3 — maximum valid, should accept (currently tested only via the filled-plugin roundtrip, not as an isolated boundary case). Pydantic's ge/le handles these correctly, but per ELSPETH Tier-1 crash-on-anomaly culture, the test suite should be the explicit documentation of the valid domain. The fact that the plan defines the domain as {1, 2, 3} is a design choice worth pinning in tests.
evidence: 16a-phase-7a-backend.md lines 459-469: only data_trust_tier=7 is tested as invalid
16a-phase-7a-backend.md line 545: data_trust_tier: int | None = Field(default=None, ge=1, le=3)

warnings.title: Plugin protocols not in mypy sweep — type: ignore[attr-defined] on new field accesses is load-bearing but unverified
warnings.detail: The plan's _to_summary replacement (lines 1045-1053) accesses six new fields via direct attribute access with type: ignore[attr-defined] on each, because PluginClass is typed as type[SourceProtocol] | type[TransformProtocol] | type[SinkProtocol] and these protocols do not declare the new Phase-7A fields. The plan says 'remove them if mypy doesn't require them' (line 1359). src/elspeth/contracts/plugin_protocols.py is not in the Task 6 mypy sweep (lines 1350-1356). Two consequences: (1) if the protocols are extended to include the new fields (the correct structural contract), the type: ignore comments would silently become wrong suppressions and mypy would emit an 'unused-ignore' error unless scoped precisely; (2) future authors reading plugin_protocols.py will see determinism/plugin_version/source_file_hash but not the Phase-7A fields, and may reasonably conclude the fields are not part of the plugin contract. The existing precedent (determinism etc. declared on both base classes and protocols) suggests the correct fix is to add the fields to the protocols. Adding plugin_protocols.py to the mypy sweep would surface this. This is a MINOR gap — the type: ignore approach works at runtime — but inconsistent with the structural documentation role of protocols in this codebase.
evidence: src/elspeth/contracts/plugin_protocols.py lines 92-94, 266-268, 471-473, 626-628: determinism / plugin_version / source_file_hash declared on protocols; new Phase-7A fields absent
16a-phase-7a-backend.md lines 1045-1053: six type: ignore[attr-defined] comments on new field accesses
16a-phase-7a-backend.md lines 1350-1356: mypy sweep covers base.py, schemas.py, service.py, csv_source.py — not plugin_protocols.py

nits.title: Commit message claims quarantine is inferred — contradicts plan rev-2
nits.detail: The Task 5 commit message at line 1319 reads: 'declared coerce + inferred io_read + inferred quarantine'. This is factually wrong. The rev-2 review history at line 1434 explicitly documents the design decision: quarantine is author-declared (because _on_validation_failure is a per-instance attribute), not inferred. The CSVSource canonical example at line 1216 declares audit_characteristics = frozenset({'coerce', 'quarantine'}) explicitly for this reason. The commit body in git history would contradict the implementation. Fix the commit message to read 'declared coerce + inferred io_read + declared quarantine'.
evidence: 16a-phase-7a-backend.md line 1434: rev-2 review history stating quarantine is author-declared
16a-phase-7a-backend.md line 1216: frozenset({'coerce', 'quarantine'}) declared explicitly on CSVSource

nits.task: Task 5 Step 10 — commit message and Step 8 comment
nits.title: Route test described as 'end-to-end integration test' — it is a unit test
nits.detail: The commit message at line 1317 calls the route test 'an end-to-end integration test pinning the wire shape.' tests/unit/web/catalog/test_routes.py is a unit test using SyncASGITestClient (in-process, no real server). It is not an end-to-end integration test by ELSPETH convention (those live in tests/integration/). The description is aspirational but misleading. The test does serve as a wire-shape pin, which is its value. Change 'end-to-end integration test' to 'wire-shape route test'.
evidence: 16a-phase-7a-backend.md line 143-144: plan itself correctly describes test_routes.py as 'conventionally treated as the wire-shape pin'

recommendations.title: Add a phase-rollup verification gate covering the catalog drawer E2E surface
recommendations.detail: The index plan describes a sequencing recommendation and mergeability notes but does not specify a post-16c verification gate. An existing E2E Playwright spec exists for the catalog drawer (tests/e2e/llm-provider-schema.spec.ts) and is currently fixme'd due to an unrelated bug (elspeth-dcf12c061b). Phase 7b/7c reshapes the catalog drawer substantially (PluginCard rewrite, CatalogDrawer wire-up, filter chips, shortcuts regroup, synthetic InlineChatSourceEntry). After 7c ships, the existing llm-provider-schema spec's page-object assumptions (catalog-page.ts using .catalog-drawer locator) should be verified against the new DOM shape. The index should nominate either: (a) an E2E smoke that opens the drawer, confirms the CSV card shows reference content (non-null usage_when_to_use, visible trust-tier badge), and confirms the 'Use in pipeline' button is absent; or (b) an explicit statement that E2E coverage for the drawer shape is the responsibility of 16c's final task. Without this, Phase 7 ships a reshaped public-facing surface with no E2E smoke test that verifies the new shape works end-to-end.
evidence: 16-phase-7-catalog-reshape.md: no mention of Playwright, E2E, or spec.ts anywhere
tests/e2e/llm-provider-schema.spec.ts lines 20-22: test.fixme'd; tests the catalog drawer

recommendations.title: Fake plugin fixture for CatalogServiceImpl tests may need an escape hatch documented more precisely
recommendations.detail: The plan's Note on test fakes (lines 1009-1021) documents that CatalogServiceImpl.__init__ eagerly calls _populate_schema_cache, and that fakes returning {} / None for get_config_schema() / get_config_model() should work cleanly. This was verified: _knob_schema returns {'fields': []} when config_model is None (service.py line 128), and validate_knob_schema with an empty fields list passes without iterations. The plan's fallback suggestion ('swap to a minimal real plugin class as the fixture') is sound but leaves ambiguity about whether the fakes as written actually work. Consider strengthening the note to say 'confirmed: _knob_schema returns {"fields": []} for config_model=None (service.py line 127-128); no swap needed.' This prevents an implementer from second-guessing and reaching for real plugin classes unnecessarily.

recommendations.title: Integration sweep does not exercise list_sources() / list_transforms() / list_sinks() against real plugins
recommendations.detail: The Task 6 Step 2 integration sweep runs tests/integration/web/ which includes tests/integration/web/catalog/test_startup_lowering.py. That test calls CatalogServiceImpl(plugin_manager) and asserts schema_cache is populated — but it does not call list_sources(), list_transforms(), or list_sinks(). After Task 4 ships _to_summary with direct field accesses on the new Phase-7A attributes, an integration test exercising the list methods against real (post-Task-1) plugins would provide stronger confidence. The tests/unit/web/catalog/test_routes.py TestListSources class covers this via the real plugin_manager fixture, so coverage is not zero. Consider adding a one-line assertion to test_startup_lowering.py: assert all(s.audit_characteristics is not None for s in svc.list_sources()) — or similar — to confirm the new _to_summary path runs against the full real plugin set. Low priority given test_routes.py coverage.

test_strategy_assessment.overall: TDD-shaped with genuine RED-GREEN-commit discipline across all five feature tasks. Each task creates a failing test before the implementation. Granularity is appropriate: derivation logic (Task 3) has its own focused suite separate from service wiring (Task 4). Wire-shape pinning (Task 5 Step 8) closes the gap between unit tests and the HTTP surface.

gaps.gap: SEEDED and NON_DETERMINISTIC enum values not tested — see WARN-1
data_trust_tier boundary values (0, -1, 1, 3) not explicitly tested — see WARN-2
test_startup_lowering.py does not call list_sources/list_transforms/list_sinks — see REC-3
No E2E verification gate for the reshaped drawer — see REC-1

positive_findings: Quarantine-cannot-be-inferred regression guard (test_quarantine_inference_is_not_attempted_from_instance_attribute) — directly guards the bug identified in rev-2. Excellent defensive test.
test_returns_sorted_tuple confirms wire-format determinism. Combined with Pydantic emitting list from tuple, this prevents a surprise sort-order bug when the frontend does indexOf comparisons.
test_plugin_summary_rejects_extra_fields guards _StrictResponse extra='forbid' contract explicitly, not just by inheritance assumption.
Task 4 fake-plugin note explicitly acknowledges that CatalogServiceImpl.__init__ is eager and documents the escape hatch. Test authors who encounter a validator rejection will know where to look.

test_csv_source_has_when_to_use / _when_not_to_use both assert len > 40 — a content-quality guard that prevents empty-string stubs from passing, which matters because the prose is the user-facing value.

observability_assessment.note: Phase 7A is a schema extension with no new decision events, no new runtime data flows, and no new error paths that require audit entries. All existing plugin loading is Tier-1 system code that crashes on anomaly rather than logging. No new observability hooks are needed for this phase.

error_path_observability.path: AttributeError on direct plugin_cls.capability_tags access in _to_summary
error_path_observability.channel: Crash propagates through CatalogServiceImpl initialization; FastAPI startup fails
error_path_observability.status: ACCEPTABLE — crash is the correct response per CLAUDE.md Plugin Ownership. The catalog service is initialized at startup, so a malformed plugin base class is caught immediately.

error_path_observability.path: Pydantic ValidationError on PluginSummary construction with bad data_trust_tier
error_path_observability.status: ACCEPTABLE — this is a Tier-1 anomaly; crash is correct. No logging needed.

xss.status: CLEAN — all new prose fields are static class attributes (Tier-1 system code). No user input path leads to these fields. The frontend renders them in text nodes (React JSX), not dangerouslySetInnerHTML.

injection.status: CLEAN — capability_tags and audit_characteristics are frozenset/tuple of string literals from plugin class bodies. No external input reaches these fields.

trust_boundary_compliance.status: CORRECT — plan explicitly classifies the new fields as Tier 1 (lines 89-97). _StrictResponse strict=True guard already applies. The plan correctly notes that the Tier-1 guard means a malformed plugin attribute crashes at PluginSummary construction, not silently degrades.

plugin_with_no_metadata.plan_handling: Correct: base class defaults (None / empty frozenset / empty tuple) ensure unfilled plugins round-trip cleanly. test_plugin_summary_defaults_for_unfilled_plugin covers this.

plugin_with_partial_metadata.plan_handling: Optional fields allow any subset. Pydantic model construction accepts any combination of None and non-None for the optional fields.
plugin_with_partial_metadata.test_coverage: Not explicitly tested as a separate case, but the defaults test covers the all-None case and the filled test covers the all-filled case. Partial is transitively covered by the Pydantic validation model.

malformed_metadata_wrong_type.plan_handling: Tier-1 crash — _StrictResponse strict=True rejects wrong-type inputs. test_plugin_summary_rejects_invalid_trust_tier covers one wrong-type case.
malformed_metadata_wrong_type.test_coverage: Partial — only data_trust_tier=7 tested. See WARN-2 for boundary values.

quarantine_attribute_class_vs_instance.plan_handling: Excellent. The plan identifies the instance-attribute problem, documents it in the derivation helper docstring, adds a dedicated regression guard test (test_quarantine_inference_is_not_attempted_from_instance_attribute), and makes quarantine author-declared. This is the design's most subtle edge case and is handled correctly.

determinism_enum_completeness.plan_handling: All six values mapped in _DETERMINISM_TO_AUDIT_FLAG. Four of six tested.
determinism_enum_completeness.test_coverage: Partial — see WARN-1 for SEEDED and NON_DETERMINISTIC

frozenset_serialization_order.plan_handling: Correctly addressed: _derive_audit_characteristics sorts before returning tuple. test_returns_sorted_tuple pins this. The risk table acknowledges the issue.

capability_tags_mutable_default.plan_handling: Using ClassVar[tuple[str, ...]] not list — immutable by default. test_capability_tags_is_a_tuple_not_a_list pins this explicitly.

defensive_programming.assessment: ACCEPTABLE — .get() on a dict is not defensive programming; it is standard Python dict access for an optional lookup. The caller knows the returned flag may be None (no mapping for that value) and handles it by not adding to inferred. This is not a hasattr or try-except hedge.

ACCEPTABLE at runtime — direct attribute access is used correctly. The type: ignore is a static-analysis workaround for the protocol-vs-base gap, not defensive programming. See WARN-3 for the recommendation to add the fields to protocols instead.

anti_patterns_assessment.layer_imports: Task 6 Step 5 runs enforce_tier_model.py check. The new import (elspeth.contracts.enums.Determinism into elspeth.web.catalog.service) is L3→L0 — within the permitted import direction. No violations expected.
anti_patterns_assessment.freeze_guards: Not applicable — no frozen dataclass instances introduced. PluginSummary is a Pydantic model, not a frozen dataclass.
anti_patterns_assessment.tdd_ordering: CORRECT — in all five feature tasks: failing test created first, implementation added, test run green, commit. No task adds implementation before the test.

production_readiness_summary.error_handling: CORRECT — crash-on-malformed-plugin-attribute is the intended behavior. No silent degradation paths introduced.
production_readiness_summary.logging: NOT APPLICABLE — no new decision events. Existing audit/telemetry channels unchanged.
production_readiness_summary.configuration: CLEAN — the new fields are class-attribute constants, not runtime configuration. No dynaconf keys introduced.
production_readiness_summary.graceful_degradation: PRESENT — all new PluginSummary fields are optional with documented fallback ('see the technical description' in the frontend). A plugin with no prose authored still serves a valid PluginSummary.
production_readiness_summary.rollback_plan: NOT ADDRESSED in the plan. For a backend schema extension with optional fields: rollback = revert the PR and redeploy. The frontend ignores unknown fields (standard JSON consumer behavior), so 16a can be reverted independently of 16b/16c. Acceptable for this scope.
production_readiness_summary.backward_compatibility: CORRECT — new fields on PluginSummary are optional with defaults. Existing frontend consumers ignore unknown fields. The plan explicitly states this and the Risks table addresses it.
